question: Q1: AI Tools in Use

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve tried Gemini (Code Assist, Studio, or CLI).
sections.hint: Google’s AI assistant, integrated into Android Studio, Firebase, and Flutter AI Toolkit
no_hands: What has held you back from trying Gemini?
Do you feel other tools already cover your needs?
If you were to try it, what task would you start with?
hands_up: What do you mainly use Gemini for — code, debugging, planning, or something else?
How smooth has the IDE or CLI experience been for you?
What’s one feature that stood out, positively or negatively?
Have you run into limits with context size, speed, or accuracy?
follow_ups: Did Gemini noticeably speed up your Flutter work?
Has anyone used Gemini together with Firebase or Flutter AI Toolkit?
What’s one tip that helps you get better results with Gemini?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used Cursor.
sections.hint: AI-first IDE with Bugbot, agent mode, and repo-wide context
no_hands: What has held you back from giving Cursor a try?
Do you feel comfortable with your current IDE setup?
What would make you consider testing it?
hands_up: How does Cursor feel compared to Copilot, Gemini, or other assistants?
Have you tried Bugbot or agent mode — how did that go?
How well does Cursor handle Flutter projects in your experience?
What’s the biggest benefit — and biggest challenge — of using it?
follow_ups: Did anyone try Cursor and then return to another IDE — why?
Has anyone used Cursor alongside other AI tools?
Who has a trick that makes Cursor work especially well for Flutter?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used Claude Code.
sections.hint: Terminal-based tool for repo-wide edits, code exploration, docs, and external data via MCP servers
no_hands: What made you hesitate to try Claude Code?
Do you prefer graphical tools over terminal-based ones?
If you were to try it, would you start with code editing, docs, or connecting external data (via MCP)?
hands_up: How do you mainly use Claude Code — CLI, IDE integration, or with MCP for external contexts?
What does Claude do better than other assistants you’ve tried?
Has anyone used it on a whole Flutter repo — how did that go?
What issues have you run into (accuracy, speed, setup)?
follow_ups: Has anyone used Claude for docs, design, or planning instead of just code?
Did Claude ever influence how you structure your project?
Who has a “hidden gem” feature or workflow in Claude to share?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve tried Copilot or other tools (e.g., Firebase Studio AI, Amazon Q).
no_hands: What factors led you to not try Copilot or similar tools yet?
Do you feel they’re too basic, too costly, or simply not needed?
If you were to try one, which task would you want it to handle first?
hands_up: How does Copilot compare to newer tools like Gemini or Cursor in your work?
Did you use it daily, or more occasionally?
What was the biggest productivity boost you noticed?
Any concerns with quality, trust, or integration in Flutter projects?
follow_ups: Has anyone replaced Copilot with another tool?
Did Copilot integrate well into your Flutter workflow?
Who here is still using multiple AI tools side by side?

wrap_up: If you had to recommend one AI tool for Flutter devs here, which would it be and why?
If you had to warn against one, which would it be and why?
What’s the biggest surprise you’ve had when experimenting with AI assistants?
